channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: 9 Arrested After BJP-TMC Clash Outside Kolkata Police Station Amid Election Campaigning
item.description: Tensions escalated outside Ekbalpur Police Station in Kolkata on Monday as a clash broke out between supporters of the BJP and the TMC, resulting in the arrest of nine individuals. The incident occurr...

item.title: New KMPDC Board Inaugurated as Kenya Strengthens Health Sector Reforms
item.description: The Ministry of Health in Kenya has officially inaugurated a new board for the Kenya Medical Practitioners and Dentists Council (KMPDC) following the expiry of the previous council's term. The ceremon...

item.title: Nigerian Wrestler Oba Femi Triumphs Over Brock Lesnar at WrestleMania
item.description: Kenya, and indeed the world, witnessed a historic moment on April 19, 2026, as Nigerian wrestler Oba Femi, known in the ring as 'The Ruler', defeated WWE legend Brock Lesnar at WrestleMania 42. The yo...

item.title: Saskatchewan Launches Its First Legal Clinic for Black Communities in Saskatoon
item.description: The Truly Alive Youth And Family Foundation Inc. (TAYFFI) in Saskatoon has launched Saskatchewan's first Black legal clinic, named Leading Change Justice Navigation for Black Adults. The clinic aims t...

item.title: Poilievre's Support Declines Among Conservative Voters: Latest Poll Shows Erosion
item.description: Recent polling by Angus Reid shows that Pierre Poilievre's popularity as the leader of the Conservative Party has decreased, despite maintaining a relatively strong following. The new poll, conducted ...

item.title: How Poor Scope Management Makes Freelancers Feel Undervalued
item.description: This article explains how many freelancers end up feeling underpaid and frustrated due to poor control of project scope. It shows that what starts as a simple job can slowly grow into a heavy workload...

item.title: Rebel Wilson sued for defamation over dispute regarding writing credit on 'The Deb'
item.description: Rebel Wilson is facing a defamation lawsuit brought by Charlotte MacInnes, lead actor in the musical comedy 'The Deb', following a dispute over sexual harassment claims and writing credits. MacInnes f...

item.title: Carney’s messaging offers reassurance, but rising inflation clouds Canada’s economic outlook
item.description: In this opinion piece, John Ivison examines Prime Minister Mark Carney’s recent “fireside chat” video, portraying it as a polished but ultimately insufficient response to Canada’s worsening economic c...

item.title: Uganda and Congo Forces Free 200 Hostages from Islamic State Group Camp
item.description: In a joint military operation, forces from Uganda and the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) rescued over 200 civilians held captive by the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), an armed group aligned with ...

item.title: Lahore High Court Grants Bail to PTI Activist Falak Javed in Fake Video Case
item.description: On April 21, 2026, the Lahore High Court granted post-arrest bail to PTI social media activist Falak Javed in connection with the case involving the viral circulation of a fake video about Punjab Info...

item.title: UK government denies entry to controversial anti-Islam activist over public order concerns
item.description: The UK Home Office has barred US-based influencer Valentina Gomez from entering the country, citing concerns that her presence would not be conducive to the public good. Gomez, who has previously run ...

item.title: The Tragic Death of Bikram Lama: A Homeless Man Lying Unnoticed for Days in Sydney’s St James Tunnel
item.description: Bikram Lama, known as the ‘birdman’ of St James tunnel in Sydney, tragically died after spending days unnoticed in one of the busiest areas of the city. Lama, a former international student from Nepal...

item.title: Hollywood Films About Rescue Missions Amidst US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The recent escalations in tensions between the United States and Iran have sparked a surge in public interest regarding rescue missions and geopolitical conflicts. The original headline, intended to g...

item.title: DepEd Expands Summer Programs to Help Students Improve Learning Skills
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) is set to implement a wider range of summer remediation programs from May 6 to June 3, 2026, aiming to assist learners who are at risk of lagging behind. Education ...

item.title: Ondo Commissioner Faces Scrutiny Over Police Investigation, Alleged Misuse of State Funds
item.description: The Ondo State Commissioner for Finance, Omowumi Isaac, has sparked controversy after being seen supervising what many describe as 'wasteful and questionable' state-funded projects. This comes after s...

item.title: Clash at Gurdwara in Germany Leaves 11 Injured Amid Disputes over Management
item.description: A violent clash at a gurdwara in Moers, Germany, resulted in 11 people being injured on Sunday. The altercation, which involved more than 40 individuals, was triggered by a disagreement over the manag...

item.title: House Republicans Block Extension of Warrantless Surveillance Program
item.description: In a late-night vote on Friday, a group of House Republicans defied party leadership and President Donald Trump by blocking a proposal to extend Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Ac...

item.title: 100 Thoughtful Good Morning Messages to Strengthen Your Relationship
item.description: This article shares a collection of 100 good morning messages designed to help people express love and appreciation to their partners, especially women. The main idea be say small, consistent acts of ...

item.title: Kenya Opens New Legal Aid Centre in Nakuru to Support Prisoners and Vulnerable Communities
item.description: The government of Kenya has launched its fourth legal aid service centre, located at the Nakuru GK Command, aimed at improving access to justice for prisoners and marginalised communities. Attorney Ge...
